title: asplenium trichomanes subsp. humistratum
intro: Asplenium trichomanes subsp. humistratum, also known as the rusty-back fern or rusty-back spleenwort, is a subspecies of fern belonging to the Asplenium genus and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which can help in soothing digestive issues and promoting wound healing. The health benefits of Asplenium trichomanes subsp. humistratum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
